item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this "series" you've got going should start with one well rounded and written file and then write tales with it, as scips really shouldn't be a series.

Why exactly can't SCP articles be parts of a series? This has been done numerous, numerous times before on this site and isn't something new. This is almost standard practice. And no, I will not write only tales after this in the series, that's neither how it should be nor something I want as a writer.

this feels closer to a Fifthist church scip than a sarkic one.

This article has nothing to do with Sarkicism. All of the runes and magic provided in this article are based on Sarkicism; the characters literally point it out by saying it is not Sarkic, but a system based on Sarkic magic - the article does not even have the 'sarkic' tag, meaning it has nothing to do with Sarkicism. Also, if I may ask, how does this feel like a Fifthist article?

I feel like you tried to throw too much into one file without properly fleshing anything out or connecting them all properly

Every single element in this article somehow connects to something else in it and makes sense. Every single element in this article is as fleshed out as I wanted to be for the current state of the story. You are supposed to be somewhat shaggy-doged at the end and wanting to go for more, which is provided.

Not to mention the regular break in clinical tone and the multiple SPAG mistakes.

Could you provide any examples of such? Just saying that without saying anything else is not constructive criticism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like, the pieces are all there for this to be a great scip, but nothing proper was done with them.
Not to mention, at certain points, this feels closer to a Fifthist church scip than a sarkic one. It's too all over the place at times, what with nothing being pinned down properly. I feel like you tried to throw too much into one file without properly fleshing anything out or connecting them all properly. I feel like this "series" you've got going should start with one well rounded and written file and then write tales with it, as scips really shouldn't be a series.
Not to mention the regular break in clinical tone and the multiple SPAG mistakes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of the elements appear again. All of the elements are important and somehow make sense. It seems you just misunderstood the article.
Also, not all cultists are dead. Only the some of the ones that carried out the ritual here - the rest of them are alive and well, as you can see by the continuation literally linked in the ending paragraphs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm by no means an expert in SCP writing, but as far as I'm concerned this doesn't work for one main reason: The article introduces cool elements, but does nothing with them:

  • The phrase at the beginning personnel is supposed to repeat
  • The requirements to observe the anomaly (being irreligious, losing a friend, etc.)
  • The MTF that is sent in and just vanishes
  • Sarkic runes
  • Entities inside being hostile but also asking for help (?)

All these elements could work on their own, but are just mentioned and don't get brought up again, so the article overall feels directionless.

Also, at the end its implied that the cult wanted to perform multiple rituals all over Poland, but it kinda loses impact when you remember they they have all been dead since the forties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know they aren't actual Polish names; they are translations of these names for the English Foundation database. I'm Polish too. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpick - there are no such Polish names as "Damien", "Herbert" or "Annie" (there is "Damian" and "Ania" though, "Herbert" could be replaced by "Henryk" if you want it to sound more Polish). Also, "Tysiąclecie Górne" sounds rather unnatural for a name of district or village, but technically isn't incorrect, so it may be just me.

The last map suggests that there are going to be at least four more skips and/or tales set in Poland - as a Pole I can provide you with proof-reading if you want to, y'know, "polish" them before. (sorry for lame pun)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's the code for the table I used in the article.

+ show block
– hide block
 [[table style="width: 100%;"]] [[row]] [[cell style="border-bottom: 1px solid #AAA; border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 90%]]**Assigned Site**[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="border-bottom: 1px solid #AAA; border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 90%]]**Site Director**[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="border-bottom: 1px solid #AAA; border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 90%]]**Research Head**[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="border-bottom: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 90%]]**Assigned Task Force**[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[/row]] [[row]] [[cell style="border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 80%]]Site-120[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 80%]]N/A[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="border-right: 1px solid #AAA; text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 80%]]Daniel Asheworth[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[cell style="text-align: center; width: 25%;"]] [[size 80%]]Omega-5 ("The Good Boys")[[/size]] [[/cell]] [[/row]] [[/table]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
completely off topic but what's the code for the ,Assigned site, site director, lead scientist, assigned task force, i cant f*cking find it lmao

+1 for your trouble

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small nitpick, there are too many 5936-1’s

  • event
  • Addendum
  • Exploration
  • Translation
  • Entity designation

Honestly it’s easy to get them mixed up.

Additionally I see almost no connection to Sarkicism, and I’m not sure why the runes were labeled as such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with a lot of what was said here; most of my concerns regarding the build-up of atmosphere, odd word choices, and relatively clunky dialogue for some parts remain, which failed to pique my interest in enjoying the whole chaotic, cult ritualistic deletion of an entire city district from common knowledge and the oncoming body-swapping ordeal in its entirety.

Reiterating, I like the setting and what possibly happened; just wasn't explored to its fullest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding the above comment. It summarizes my thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't work for me for a variety of reasons.

+ show block
– hide block

The description contradicts itself , there are various clinical tone breaks, the mtf dialogue is cliche and bordering on cheesy, the exploration logs didn't do anything particularly cool or interesting and didn't sustain tension, the diary entries were melodramatic, etc, etc.

Most disappointingly of all, the hook of having whoever enters use the phrase before entering was not used and did not play into the story at any point in time. That was a very good hook that was unfortunately abandoned. Another disappointment was the failure to explore this sarkic-abrahamic fusion you've got going on. It wasnt built upon or used for anything, which is a shame because you could have come up with a unique mythos to explore.

From what I understand this is the first in a series, but SCP articles should be able to stand alone.

item.title: The First Seal has been broken
item.content:encoded:
This is a rewrite of an old article by myself bearing the same name and the same number slot. Since it's a series starter, and the previous version was rather mediocre looking back, I decided to rewrite it a bit.

Part two is coming soon(ish).

Special thanks to these people for checking it out before I post:
AnActualCrow | Dysadron | Pedagon | Veralta | Meisteroni | RadioactiveRADS

The Department of Ontokinetics logo used in the header was created by EstrellaYoshte for the purpose of all articles regarding such. Please check out more of their absolutely stellar work here.

~ also check out the ralliston coolpage funpage for more garbage ~
